use std::fs::File;
use std::io::{BufWriter, Write};

fn write_lines(path: &str, lines: &[&str]) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let file = File::create(path)?;
    let mut writer = BufWriter::new(file);
    for line in lines {
        writeln!(writer, "{}", line)?;
    }
    writer.flush()?;
    Ok(())
}

fn write_records(path: &str, records: &[Record]) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let file = File::create(path)?;
    let mut writer = BufWriter::new(file);
    for record in records {
        writeln!(writer, "{},{},{}", record.id, record.name, record.value)?;
    }
    writer.flush()?;
    Ok(())
}

fn append_log(path: &str, entries: &[String]) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let file = File::options().append(true).open(path)?;
    let mut writer = BufWriter::new(file);
    for entry in entries {
        writeln!(writer, "{}", entry)?;
    }
    writer.flush()?;
    Ok(())
}

fn write_json_lines(path: &str, items: &[serde_json::Value]) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let file = File::create(path)?;
    let mut writer = BufWriter::new(file);
    for item in items {
        serde_json::to_writer(&mut writer, item)?;
        writer.write_all(b"\n")?;
    }
    writer.flush()?;
    Ok(())
}

struct Record { id: u64, name: String, value: String }
